context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce

episode_text:
day=26; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Social with person in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals, now including a simple bite-sized sandwich meal, support a moderate reheating baseline. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE social savory meals across multiple partner locations, now including a front-social fish-and-potatoes meal, consistently support moderate reheating. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 1 min BECAUSE cumulative working-meal evidence centers on milder reheating for savory foods. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce and fruit, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed right-working dessert-like meal supports somewhat more reheating for that mixed sweet context than the user's usual savory-working pattern. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected moderate reheating for dessert-like items in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory meal indicates a stronger reheating preference there than in other contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory evidence supports a moderate reheating preference. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN 1 min BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals support a milder reheating preference in that context. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit or fruit with a cold topping, THEN no microwave BECAUSE personal fruit meals continue to support leaving cold items unheated.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this in right-TV contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly to preserve visibility toward the social partner. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-social meals show the same side-matched visibility preference seen in other attended-target contexts. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-social meals, now including a savory meal with tartar sauce, show the user wants the robot to preserve the front view during face-to-face interaction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-working meals consistently show the user wants the robot to preserve the front view while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals continue to show the user aligns occlusion avoidance with the attended device location. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to preserve visibility toward the TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals indicate occlusion concerns track the specific attended target and are not purely side-based across all work contexts. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals, including the new simple sandwich meal, are consistent with the baseline that occlusion matters mainly when there is a salient person or screen to keep visible.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a moderate speed for a messier meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN slow BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user prefers more cautious motion for a hotter, more substantial meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, now including a front-social fish-bites meal, support a balanced speed preference. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed across observed working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN medium BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence supports a balanced speed preference there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals reinforce a moderate speed preference. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated personal meals across sweet and savory foods continue to support a stable moderate baseline rather than either maximal speed or extra caution.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid savory foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites for savory solids across personal, social, TV, and working meals, now including another front-social savory meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies and banana pieces, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed episode supports the same dessert-related exception where the user accepts different acquisition geometry to improve pickup success for soft sweets. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit with topping, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the personal strawberries-with-whipped-cream meal extends the pattern that the user accepts perpendicular skewering for soft sweet items where pickup reliability may matter more than minimizing bite width.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, now including a front-social fish-bites meal, support confirmation in social contexts, consistent with wanting to avoid awkward failed transfers. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is more complex fork-fed meals or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN yes BECAUSE several working meals support a preference for explicit bite-acquisition verification when attention is divided or bite acquisition may be messier. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a straightforward savory finger-food-style meal, THEN no BECAUSE the front-laptop buffalo chicken meal showed the user may skip confirmation in simpler working contexts to reduce interaction time. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for a more complex, messier bite-acquisition context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user also wants explicit verification for substantial savory foods. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support confirmation in that more complex feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals show the user is comfortable skipping confirmation in easier feeding contexts there. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food with topping, hot savory fork-fed food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated personal meals now include both substantial and simple savory foods and continue to support explicit bite-acquisition verification even outside social or work settings. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit verification and faster flow AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simpler meal, THEN favor no when the overall interaction style is streamlined, with short autocontinue waits and low-mess food, and favor yes when the meal is messier, more delicate, more substantial, or otherwise manually controlled BECAUSE the cumulative evidence suggests the user trades confirmation for efficiency mainly in easier contexts outside the personal baseline.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and repeatedly selected the less intrusive transfer style in personal meals, including a simple bite-sized sandwich meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, now including a front-social fish-bites meal, support the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive transfer style across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected outside-mouth transfer across those front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows that for a more substantial savory TV meal the user preferred reduced self-motion over the usual less-intrusive style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals match the user's broader preference for user-controlled, less intrusive delivery. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence indicates that even there the user generally prefers the less intrusive, user-controlled style. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated personal meals reinforce that the user generally prefers to lean in rather than have the robot enter the mouth when the context is calm.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in observed personal meals, including both savory meals and a simple bite-sized sandwich meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, now including a front-social fish-bites meal, support a somewhat larger buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance across working meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN near BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals reinforce a close stop preference. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN near BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence provides direct support for a close stop distance there. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is any observed state AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when inside-mouth transfer is chosen.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, including the new front-social meal, show a stable preference for quieter signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose LED during working meals, consistent with low-disruption visual cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN LED BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence aligns that context with the user's broader TV and work preference for quiet visual prompting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN LED BECAUSE observed right-TV meals show the user can prefer quiet visual prompting there as well. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user wants a more salient multimodal cue in quiet personal settings when there is no concern about disturbing others or media.

IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by meal complexity and overall control style, favoring button when the user also chooses confirmation, longer autocontinue waits, and other manual-control settings, and autocontinue only in isolated simpler TV meals BECAUSE the user's broader pattern strongly favors explicit control.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated social meals show the user prioritizes reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to a social partner is important, and the additional front-social savory meal reinforces this for face-to-face interaction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-working meals show the user wants the robot to clear the front view between tasks. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals provide direct evidence that when the attended device is on the right, the user wants the robot to clear that view between tasks. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show the user wants the robot to clear the left-side view between tasks. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized pizza or other savory TV meals, THEN yes BECAUSE one right-TV meal shows the user may want the robot to clear the right-side view when preserving TV visibility is important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals show the user does not want the extra time cost of retracting there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE an earlier right-TV meal shows the user may tolerate continued arm presence to reduce meal time. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals, including the new simple sandwich meal, reinforce the baseline preference to avoid extra time cost when there is no external person or screen whose view must be preserved. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving visibility and reducing time AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is attended-target contexts, THEN tie-break by whether the user is prioritizing uninterrupted visibility and explicit control, favoring yes when the meal also uses confirmation, longer waits, and other manual-control settings, and favoring no when the user is accepting faster, more streamlined service or when no salient external target needs protection BECAUSE the observed right-side TV, right-laptop, front-social, and personal meals fit that pattern.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE observed social meals on both sides and now in front support a moderate, mess-conscious dipping preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left or right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping across several side-working meals with ranch dressing, ketchup, and chocolate sauce, supporting a moderate, mess-conscious working preference there. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE front-working savory evidence includes meals where the user explicitly declined available sauce, suggesting a stronger no-dip tendency when working directly in front, likely to reduce mess and distraction. IF food does not include a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed savory working meals reinforce that the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping when the sauce was integral to the meal. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal with ranch dressing, THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support moderate dipping rather than no dipping or heavy dipping. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative, including personal and front-social savory meals without dipping. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style meal without dipping use, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal is consistent with the user's conservative no-dip baseline when dipping is not needed. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit or dessert-like food with a topping or dip, THEN less BECAUSE the personal strawberries-with-whipped-cream meal shows the user accepts some dipping in a quiet personal sweet-food context without escalating to maximal dipping. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal without sauce or dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals, now including a bite-sized sandwich meal, reinforce the conservative no-dip baseline when dipping is unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated social meals across partner locations, now including another front-social savory meal, show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when conversation or social attention may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left or right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very long wait while working in side-device contexts, reinforcing a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a more manually controlled or complex meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier front-working meals supported a very long intervention window when the user also chose confirmation and other explicit-control settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a straightforward savory meal with a more streamlined interaction style, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the front-laptop buffalo chicken meal showed the user may accept short waits in simpler working contexts when also skipping confirmation and favoring efficiency. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode indicates an intermediate wait preference: more time than highly automated TV meals, but less than the very long waits used in work or socially distracting contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence shows the user may want a long intervention window there when choosing a more manually controlled interaction style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized savory food, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence shows the user may want a very long intervention window there when choosing a more manually controlled interaction style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the earlier observed meal reinforces a short wait preference in TV-viewing contexts where the user accepts more automation. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft fruit, dessert-like food, hot savory food with confirmation and other manual-control settings, or simple bite-sized savory food, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated personal meals, now including a simple sandwich meal, support an intermediate wait in quiet personal contexts when the user wants some intervention time but not the extremely long delays used in social or highly distracting settings. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving user intervention time and reducing delay AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is attended-target or manually controlled meals, THEN tie-break by desired automation level, favoring 10 sec when the user also chose no confirmation and other streamlined settings, 100 sec for moderately manual but still efficient meals, and 1000 sec when the user wants strong protection against unwanted progression despite distraction or social demands BECAUSE the observed meals separate into these levels.
